title: Past Events & Tech Challenges
text:
Continuous Fiber Reinforced Additive Manufacturing Technologies
Deadline— February 13, 2026 (Closed)
ERDC seeks to identify and investigate emerging solutions that can help the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers incorporate continuous fiber-reinforced additive manufacturing technologies into future Civil Works infrastructure fabrication and replacement workflows.
Read MoreContinuous Fiber Reinforced Additive Manufacturing Technologies
Help Solve One of the Nation’s Top Flood Risk Management Challenges through Automated Modular Tunnels
Deadline— February 17, 2026 (Closed)
NEW OPPORTUNITY: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers is seeking insights and expertise from leading industry professionals with proven applicable experience in automated modular tunnel technologies and processes for large-scale water conveyance systems as alternatives to traditional methods.
Read MoreHelp Solve One of the Nation’s Top Flood Risk Management Challenges through Automated Modular Tunnels
Advancing Shoreline Resilience at Naval Support Area Cutler
Deadline— January 16, 2026 (Closed)
ERDC seeks expert technical support and innovation to develop a new application for natural and hybrid infrastructure (NHI) technology and methodology for NHI placement in a complex landscape in the northeastern-most region of the continental United States.
Read MoreAdvancing Shoreline Resilience at Naval Support Area Cutler
Blast Overpressure Protection
Deadline— December 22, 2025 (Closed)
ERDC seeks to obtain innovative solutions that can reduce the hazards of blast overpressure within buildings and protective structures.
Read MoreBlast Overpressure Protection
Commercial Production of Biopolymers
Deadline— January 5, 2026 (Closed)
ERDC is seeking information from industry on the ability to produce a lab-developed biopolymer made from the Rhizobium tropici (RT) bacterium.
Read MoreCommercial Production of Biopolymers
Supporting Objective Determinations of Areas That Are Not “Waters of the United States” Post-Sackett Using Geospatial Tools
Deadline— December 24, 2025 (Closed)
The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers seeks to create an innovative tool to aid in implementing the Clean Water Act post-Sackett definition of “Waters of the United States.”
Read MoreSupporting Objective Determinations of Areas That Are Not “Waters of the United States” Post-Sackett Using Geospatial Tools
Help Solve One of the Nation’s Top Flood Risk Management Challenges through Automated Modular Tunnels
Deadline— November 19, 2025 (Closed)
The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers is seeking insights and expertise from leading industry professionals with proven applicable experience in innovative approaches to repurposing existing automated modular tunnel technologies and processes for large-scale water conveyance systems as alternatives to traditional methods.
Read MoreHelp Solve One of the Nation’s Top Flood Risk Management Challenges through Automated Modular Tunnels
Transformative AI/ML High-Performance Computing
Deadline— October 16, 2025 (Closed)
ERDC seeks commercial solutions capable of delivering secure, scalable, and real-time artificial intelligence/machine learning computations in complex and operationally relevant military scenarios.
Read MoreTransformative AI/ML High-Performance Computing
Dredging Solutions for Improved Navigation
Deadline— September 24, 2025 (Closed)
ERDC seeks to identify and investigate viable solutions that reduce navigation dredging requirements at shallow and deep channels.
Read MoreDredging Solutions for Improved Navigation
Uncrewed Surface Vehicles for Nearshore Mapping
Deadline— September 19, 2025 (Closed)
ERDC seeks solutions to sustain Uncrewed Surface Vehicle (USV) transit through the surfzone in breaking waves, currents, and coastal inlets.
Read MoreUncrewed Surface Vehicles for Nearshore Mapping
Cybersecurity for Advanced Computing and Software Modernization
Deadline— September 8, 2025 (Closed)
ERDC is seeking commercial solutions that advance the state of cybersecurity for emerging and future computing paradigms to transition forward-leaning cybersecurity technologies into operationally relevant prototypes that enhance the resilience, trustworthiness, and survivability of DoD digital infrastructure.
Read MoreCybersecurity for Advanced Computing and Software Modernization
Traction Aids for Ground Vehicle Mobility
Deadline— August 15, 2025 (Closed)
ERDC seeks to identify and investigate viable solutions that improve traction for vehicles operating in austere or contested environments.
Read MoreTraction Aids for Ground Vehicle Mobility
Overtopping Breach Mitigation
Deadline— July 31, 2025 (Closed)
ERDC seeks to identify and investigate emerging solutions that can help reduce flood-induced overtopping of earthen levee embankments.
Read MoreOvertopping Breach Mitigation
Innovation 2 Market
Deadline— August 8, 2025 (Closed)
The Innovation 2 Market (I2M) Patent Showcase in Champaign, IL, will help businesses and investors pursue opportunities to introduce ERDC innovations to government and commercial markets.
Read MoreInnovation 2 Market
Webinar: Waterways Engineering
Deadline— July 14, 2025 (Closed)
ERDC's patented technologies that address challenges in waterways engineering have strong market potential. During this webinar, attendees will learn how commercialization of these technologies can be beneficial for business growth and development.
Read MoreWebinar: Waterways Engineering
